expectations.text: Recommends `winml inspect` as the cheapest first check (config-only).
expectations.evidence: 'Step 1: `winml inspect ...` This reads only the model config (no weights, no export, no hardware spin-up).'
'Step 2: `winml analyze` against the exported ONNX, targeting the OpenVINO EP.'

expectations.text: Identifies OpenVINO as EP for Intel NPU.
Explains analyze's role: linter classifies operators per EP.
expectations.evidence: 'The analyzer runs an operator-level linter and tags every op as supported / partial / unsupported for the EP you're targeting.'

expectations.text: Does NOT just say 'build and see'.
expectations.evidence: Explicit two-step pre-flight (inspect + analyze) before committing to the full build.

expectations.text: All `winml` commands quoted in the response are runnable — every flag exists in `winml <cmd> --help`, no positional model arg where the CLI is flag-only.
expectations.evidence: verify_commands.py found 1 broken command(s): - `winml inspect nvidia/segformer-b1-finetuned-ade-512-512` has positional arg(s) ['nvidia/segformer-b1-finetuned-ade-512-512'] but `winml inspect` is flag-only
